spec_match.rationale: The demo does not actually activate or use the <usermedia> element, instead calling getUserMedia() directly on button click even when pretending that the element is active.

interactive.rationale: The user can click the start camera button to trigger the webcam and stop it, with live stream info and console logging.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The explanation matches the spec's goal of declarative user-activated camera/microphone access.

edge_cases.rationale: It handles some basic error states but doesn't properly test browser support, using a flawed feature detection check that always passes.

references.rationale: The page correctly links to the parent index and the chromestatus.com entry.

design_system.rationale: It fits the visual showcase design system, using monospace cards for logs and clean button layout.

openQuestions.title: Fix feature detection for <usermedia> element
openQuestions.detail: The current check `typeof umEl.addEventListener === 'function'` is always true because all elements (including HTMLUnknownElement) have addEventListener. It should check `typeof HTMLUserMediaElement !== 'undefined'` or inspect the constructor name to ensure it's not HTMLUnknownElement.

openQuestions.title: Actually trigger native <usermedia> element activation
openQuestions.detail: The demo completely bypasses the <usermedia> element by having it hidden and calling getUserMedia() on button click. If <usermedia> is a native button-like element, the button should be the <usermedia> element itself rather than a standard button that triggers getUserMedia() in JS.

summary: The demo implements a facade that always reports <usermedia> is supported, but behind the scenes it only calls the traditional navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia() API on a hidden element.
